title: Apothecary Console
summary: A wide open-shelf console with graduated cubby dividers along its length, built from ripped plywood strips — three shelves and stepped compartments in an apothecary-drawer-style layout.
description:
This console builds its box from plywood ripped into consistent 15-1/2" strips rather than solid 1x lumber, giving it a longer, wider footprint than most consoles in this catalog while keeping material cost down. Assemble the internal partitions first, working bottom to top — each set of dividers (bottom, drawer-height, and top) gets screwed and glued to its neighbor before the whole partition unit ever meets the sides, so the compartment spacing is locked in before the box closes up around it.

Once the internal partitions are square, attach the sides to all the shelves and the top in one pass. **Check the whole assembly for square before attaching the back panel** — the plywood back, cut and nailed on last, is what a bowed or racked partition unit would show up in immediately, since it has to sit flat across the whole rear opening.

The top overhangs the front and sides by 1" but sits flush with the back edge — a small detail worth double-checking before you glue, since it's easy to center the overhang symmetrically by habit and end up with a top that doesn't sit flush against a wall. Footer trim wraps the base last, finishing the piece at floor level.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood or MDF, full sheets, ripped to 15-1/2" strips
1/4" plywood, Lauan, or MDF, full sheet
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

1/4" plywood, Lauan, or MDF, full sheet

steps.title: Build the internal partitions
steps.body: Starting at the bottom, attach the bottom dividers, then the drawer dividers, then the top dividers to their neighboring shelves with 2" screws and glue, building the internal partition structure bottom to top.
materials: 3/4" plywood or MDF, full sheets, ripped to 15-1/2" strips

steps.title: Attach the top and sides
steps.body: Attach the top board to the top partitions with 2" screws and glue, then attach the sides to all shelves and the top, checking square as you go.
materials: 3/4" plywood or MDF, full sheets, ripped to 15-1/2" strips

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Check the whole project is square, then cut the 1/4" plywood back and attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, fastening to every partition and shelf.
materials: 1/4" plywood, Lauan, or MDF, full sheet

steps.title: Add the top boards
steps.body: Fasten the top boards to the top with 1-1/4" screws and glue, keeping a 1" overhang on the front and sides and flush with the back edge.

steps.title: Add the footer
steps.body: Attach the side and front footer boards at the base, and the inset front footer, with 1-1/4" screws or nails and glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill any screw holes with wood filler, sand all surfaces, and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Wide open-shelf apothecary-style console with cubby dividers
